user_query:
Read input/profile.json, input/topics.csv, input/case_study.md, and input/contrarian.md. Use these to create a LinkedIn authority package for me. Write every deliverable under output/ at the exact paths below.

Deliverables:
1) output/posts/thought_leadership.md — 350-500 words using this 4-part structure with explicit section headers: "Hook", "Story", "Lesson", "Perspective + CTA". Personalize with my NAME and keep the TONE from the profile.
2) output/posts/insight.md — max 150 words. Format as: a strong hook line on line 1, then 3–5 short paragraphs, ending with a single closing question on the last line.
3) output/posts/case_study.md — Use the input/case_study.md details. Start with the exact pattern: "The [client type] came to me with [problem]..." and end with a clear CTA for prospects (e.g., "DM me" or "If you're...").
4) output/posts/contrarian.md — Challenge the common belief in input/contrarian.md. Be thoughtful and invite discussion. End exactly with: "Agree? Disagree? Let me know below."
5) output/articles/definitive_guide.md — 1000–1500 words. Title from the matching article topic in input/topics.csv. Structure: Introduction, at least three H2 sections (lines starting with "## "), one numbered list, 1–2 personal examples, and a conclusion with a CTA.
6) output/profile/headline.txt — Write a headline in the formula "[What you do] for [who you serve] -> [Result]" ("->" or "→" acceptable).
7) output/profile/about.md — Organize with these explicit section headers: "Hook", "Story", "Who you serve", "What you offer", "Social proof", "CTA".
8) output/outreach/connection_request.txt — A short, genuine connection request referencing the audience and including "Would love to connect".
9) output/outreach/follow_up_dm.txt — A follow-up DM that includes the phrase "No pitch — genuinely curious".
10) output/bios/speaker_bio_short.txt — ~100 words (acceptable range: 90–110).
11) output/bios/speaker_bio_full.txt — ~300 words (acceptable range: 260–340).
12) output/strategy/posting_schedule.csv — CSV with columns Day, Content Type, Best Time. Rows for Monday–Friday that match: Monday 7-8 AM, Tuesday 10-11 AM, Wednesday 7-8 AM, Thursday 12-1 PM, Friday 8-9 AM.
13) output/plan/90_day_plan.md — A 90-day plan with clearly labeled sections: "Days 1-30", "Days 31-60", "Days 61-90", each with actionable bullets.

Follow the input data closely and keep everything in English. Avoid exaggerated claims; make CTAs discussion-oriented, not spammy.
